title: State parameters (tau, eps, delta) confirmed BINDING at multi-K resolution

content.hypothesis: Entry 043 claimed state parameter binding (d-values for eps, tau, delta) but those values were fabricated by a researcher agent. Actual multi-K experiments needed to determine if tau, eps, delta are binding structural parameters for Stage 4.
content.method: Multi-K sweep K=[4,6,8,10], 5 paired seeds [42,137,2024,999,7], n_perm=4, n_trials=3 (reduced for speed). Per-cell MI measurement. Each param swept across 5 values while others held at canonical. Script: src/stage4_state_param_multik.py
content.result:
ALL THREE BINDING at multi-K resolution.

Delta (canonical=0.35):
- delta=0.1: d=-3.122, p=0.000 (strong degradation)
- delta=0.2: d=-1.384, p=0.002 (degradation)
- delta=0.5: d=+0.580, p=0.195 (borderline improvement)
- delta=0.7: d=+0.738, p=0.099 (borderline improvement)
Pattern: Monotonic. Lower mixing rate hurts significantly. Higher might help but not significant.

Eps (canonical=0.15):
- eps=0.05: d=-3.092, p=0.000 (strong degradation)
- eps=0.0: d=-0.630, p=0.159 (borderline)
- eps=0.3: d=+0.426, p=0.340
- eps=0.5: d=+0.674, p=0.131
Pattern: Non-monotonic. eps=0.05 is WORSE than eps=0.0 (weak coupling actively harmful). Higher eps trends toward improvement but nothing significant.

Tau (canonical=0.3):
- tau=0.2: d=+1.202, p=0.007 (SIGNIFICANT IMPROVEMENT)
- tau=0.1: d=-0.823, p=0.066 (borderline degradation)
- tau=0.5: d=+0.758, p=0.090 (borderline improvement)
Pattern: Inverted-U. tau=0.2 is optimal. Canonical tau=0.3 is suboptimal.

metrics.eps_0.05: d=-3.092, p=0.000 (strong degradation, worse than eps=0)
metrics.protocol_note: Reduced n_perm=4, n_trials=3 (half standard protocol). CV=7.3% vs expected 5.1%.

content.methodology_lesson: IMPORTANT CAVEAT: All significant findings are calibration (better fixed values), not adaptive opportunities. delta and eps show significant DEGRADATION in one direction only. tau=0.2 is a better fixed value than canonical tau=0.3. Finding suboptimal canonical defaults is necessary groundwork but is NOT sufficient for Stage 4 — Principle II requires self-generated adaptation signals, not designer-chosen optimal values.
content.implication: tau=0.2 is the strongest Stage 4 candidate: genuine improvement (d=+1.202, p=0.007), inverted-U landscape (non-trivial optimum), and tau controls attention temperature (structurally interpretable). Next step: design a self-generated signal that drives tau toward its optimal value per-cell.
